name: Coraline
description: 100% local, Rust code-intelligence MCP server combining embeddings, lexical search, and a code knowledge graph; installs a git hook to auto-sync its index on commit

developer-org.comment: Single-maintainer open-source project by GitHub user greysquirr3l; releases signed off by Nick Campbell.

implementation-language.comment: Rust core (~84% of repo, with small C/C++ portions from vendored deps like ONNX/tree-sitter). Installed via `cargo install coraline`.

first-release.comment: v0.1.0 published 14 Feb 2025 (earliest tag on the releases history).

status.comment: Actively released; latest v0.10.2 (18 Jun 2026), 24 releases total. No beta/preview labeling.

license.comment: Dual-licensed 'Apache License 2.0 or MIT license at your option'.

open-source.comment: Source openly available under OSI licenses (MIT/Apache-2.0).

github-stars.comment: 14 stars as of 2026-07-09 (2 forks); small single-maintainer project.

form-factor.comment: Ships as an MCP server (`coraline serve --mcp`), a CLI, and a Rust library/crate; `coraline hooks install` adds a git post-commit hook to auto-sync the index.

standalone-usable.comment: Runs independently as CLI/MCP server; can be bolted onto any MCP-capable agent, not welded into one host.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone tool, not an engine bundled inside a specific agent/IDE.

install-mechanism.comment: `cargo install coraline` or pre-built binaries from GitHub releases. (The git hook is a feature, not the install path.)

retrieval-technique.comment: Combines local ONNX vector embeddings (semantic search), lexical/symbol search, and a tree-sitter-built code/knowledge graph (call graphs, impact analysis). 'hybrid' reflects the tool's own positioning of blending these; separate MCP tools (`coraline_search` vs `coraline_semantic_search`) also exist per-mode.

query-interface.comment: Exposes ~33 MCP tools (tool-call); supports semantic natural-language search, symbol/signature lookup, and text/lexical search.

returns.comment: Returns symbol references, call hierarchies/impact analysis, and contextual code snippets; a 'compact' output mode reduces tokens.

structural-awareness.comment: Uses tree-sitter to parse code into symbols and a graph rather than treating it as plain text.

chunking-strategy.comment: tree-sitter parsing yields symbol-level units (functions/classes) for find/edit/embed operations.

reranking.comment: No documented second-stage rerank (cross-encoder/LLM/graph-rank) over first-pass hits as of 2026-07-09.

index-location.comment: SQLite-backed index stored locally; '100% local', no cloud index.

incremental-updates.comment: `coraline sync` performs incremental updates of changed files rather than a full rebuild.

real-time-watch.comment: Auto-syncs on git commit via the installed hook; trigger is the commit, not a continuous file watcher.

vector-store.comment: Embeddings and graph stored in a local SQLite database.

embedding-model.comment: Runs a local ONNX embedding model; nomic-embed-text-v1.5 is the documented default in .coraline/config.json (downloaded via `coraline model download`). Verified 2026-07-09.

mcp-support.comment: Ships an MCP server exposing ~33 code-intelligence tools (`coraline serve --mcp`).

agent-compatibility.comment: Documented integrations: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, and OpenCode; works with any MCP client. 'other' covers Claude Desktop / OpenCode which lack dedicated tags.

ide-integrations.comment: Surfaces as a CLI/terminal tool and MCP server (configured via `.claude/mcp.json` / `.opencode/config.json`); no dedicated VS Code/JetBrains extension.

language-agnostic.comment: Retrieval depends on tree-sitter parser support; limited to the ~33 supported languages.

supported-languages.value: ~33 languages via tree-sitter (TypeScript, Rust, Python, Go, C#, Java, C/C++, Ruby, Bash, PHP, Swift, Kotlin, Haskell, Scala, Lua, Markdown, and more)

code-leaves-machine.comment: '100% local — all processing happens on your machine'; local ONNX embeddings mean neither code nor embeddings egress.

local-only-option.comment: Fully offline: local embedding model + local SQLite index, no external API required.

self-hostable.comment: OSS tool that runs entirely on the user's own machine.

telemetry.comment: No telemetry documented; consistent with the 100%-local positioning. Marked none based on absence of any usage-data collection in docs/README (checked 2026-07-09).

max-repo-scale.comment: Docs position it to 'index and incrementally sync large codebases'; no explicit monorepo-scale claim.

pricing-model.comment: Free/OSS; local ONNX embeddings mean no required paid embedding API.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free and open source; no paid plan.

maturity.comment: 24 releases over ~1.5 years, reaching v0.10.x with OpenSSF Baseline certification, but still pre-1.0, single-maintainer, and low adoption — usable but evolving.

active-maintenance.comment: Latest release v0.10.2 on 18 Jun 2026, within ~90 days of the 2026-07-09 research date.

community-size.comment: ~14 GitHub stars, 2 forks; single-maintainer, very small user base.
